───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

• The subdev/ fencing change is in and root git status is now clean of those nested repos. I’m just waiting on the codex-session
  test rerun after the approval-menu fix, then I’ll summarize what’s done and what remains blocked on human-side validation.

• Waited for background terminal

• Fixed the backend test-script coverage gap. packages/backend/package.json now runs packages/backend/scripts/run-tests.mjs, which
  explicitly discovers all backend *.test.ts files and runs them sequentially. That closes the false-green case where npm run test
  -w backend only executed a subset. I updated the note in packages/backend/README.md, marked aim 439230f6-4347-4abc-a23b-
  2f67e58739bd done, added a reflection, and created follow-up aim 0227dd11-7432-49dd-ba8c-63a4d6d1bd24 for making the backend
  tests parallel-safe later.

  I also fixed the codex animator issue you hit. packages/wrapped-agents/codex-session/src/watchdog.ts now treats approval menus as
  explicit option-choice situations and supports shortcut keys like y, a, and esc instead of only sending numeric options. The
  tests in packages/wrapped-agents/codex-session/src/watchdog.test.ts cover that. I also fenced subdev/ properly in .gitignore and
  clarified it in subdev/README.md, so nested side-project repos no longer pollute root git status.

  Verification:

  - npm run test -w backend → 26 tests, all passing
  - npm run test -w wrapped-agents-codex-session → passing

  Remaining release blockers are still mostly the human-side ones: real-machine startup/port validation and validator-script
  output.
